task.title: Fix approve button multi-fire and replace reply with in-row approval badge

messages.content:
New task: Fix two related issues with the approve button in `public/app.js` and `src/dashboard.ts`. --- **Bug 1 — multi-fire:** The button click handler is wired with `addEventListener` inside `renderMessages()`, which runs on every SSE tick. `morphdom` preserves DOM nodes, but the event listeners accumulate — click once fires N times. Fix: use **event delegation** on the stable `#messages-list` container instead. Wire once at init time, not inside render. **Bug 2 — UX:** Clicking approve currently POSTs a new `"Approved."` message to the agent. Instead, it should mark the original message as approved in-place. The human sees a badge on the row, not a new chat message. --- **Implementation plan:** **DB (`src/db.ts`):** - Add migration: `ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN approved_at INTEGER` (nullable, epoch ms) - Add `approved_at: number | null` to the `Message` interface - Add a function `approveMessage(id: number): void` that sets `approved_at = strftime('%s','now') * 1000` (or `Date.now()` via JS) - Update `getRecentMessages` — `SELECT *` already picks it up, no query change needed **API (`src/dashboard.ts`):** - Add `POST /api/messages/:id/approve` endpoint that calls `approveMessage(id)` and returns `{ ok: true }` - Remove or keep the existing `POST /api/messages` endpoint (keep it — still needed for manual human messages) **Frontend (`public/app.js`):** - Remove the `querySelectorAll('.msg-approve-btn').forEach(btn => btn.addEventListener(...))` block inside `renderMessages` - Add a single delegated listener on `#messages-list` at init time (outside render): ```js document.getElementById('messages-list').addEventListener('click', async (e) => { const btn = e.target.closest('.msg-approve-btn'); if (!btn) return; btn.disabled = true; btn.textContent = '✓ approved'; await fetch(`/api/messages/${btn.dataset.msgId}/approve`, { method: 'POST' }); }); ``` - In the message row renderer (the `approveBtn` variable), render the approve button only when `!m.approved_at` and `m.needs_approval`. When `m.approved_at` is set, render a static badge instead: ```js const approveBtn = (!fromHuman && m.needs_approval) ? (m.approved_at ? `
✓ approved
` : `
`) : ''; ``` Note: remove `data-to` and `data-priority` from the button — the new endpoint only needs `msg-id`. **CSS (`public/app.css` or inline):** add `.msg-approved-badge` style — muted text, similar visual weight to the disabled button state. **Files to edit:** `src/db.ts`, `src/dashboard.ts`, `public/app.js`, `public/app.css`. **Build:** `npm run build` must pass. Commit. Report DONE to cec50b17:0.

title: Fix approve button multi-fire and replace reply with in-row approval badge
label: good

Fix: use **event delegation** on the stable `#messages-list` container instead. Wire once at init time, not inside render. **Bug 2 — UX:** Clicking approve currently POSTs a new `"Approved."` message to the agent. Instead, it should mark the original message as approved in-place. The human sees a badge on the row, not a new chat message. --- **Implementation plan:** **DB (`src/db.ts`):** - Add migration: `ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN approved_at INTEGER` (nullable, epoch ms) - Add `approved_at: number | null` to the `Message` interface - Add a function `approveMessage(id: number): void` that sets `approved_at = strftime('%s','now') * 1000` (or `Date.now()` via JS) - Update `getRecentMessages` — `SELECT *` already picks it up, no query change needed **API (`src/dashboard.ts`):** - Add `POST /api/messages/:id/approve` endpoint that calls `approveMessage(id)` and returns `{ ok: true }` - Remove or keep the existing `POST /api/messages` endpoint (keep it — still needed for manual human messages) **Frontend (`public/app.js`):** - Remove the `querySelectorAll('.msg-approve-btn').forEach(btn => btn.addEventListener(...))` block inside `renderMessages` - Add a single delegated listener on `#messages-list` at init time (outside render): ```js document.getElementById('messages-list').addEventListener('click', async (e) => { const btn = e.target.closest('.msg-approve-btn'); if (!btn) return; btn.disabled = true; btn.textContent = '✓ approved'; await fetch(`/api/messages/${btn.dataset.msgId}/approve`, { method: 'POST' }); }); ``` - In the message row renderer (the `approveBtn` variable), render the approve button only when `!m.approved_at` and `m.needs_approval`. When `m.approved_at` is set, render a static badge instead: ```js const approveBtn = (!fromHuman && m.needs_approval) ? (m.approved_at ? `
✓ approved
` : `
`) : ''; ``` Note: remove `data-to` and `data-priority` from the button — the new endpoint only needs `msg-id`. **CSS (`public/app.css` or inline):** add `.msg-approved-badge` style — muted text, similar visual weight to the disabled button state. **Files to edit:** `src/db.ts`, `src/dashboard.ts`, `public/app.js`, `public/app.css`. **Build:** `npm run build` must pass. Commit. Report DONE to cec50b17:0.
